Name: neorv32_xbus.xbus_adr_o
Description: Drives the 32-bit target address on the external Wishbone-compatible bus derived from the internal transaction address (bus_req.addr).
Justification: xbus_adr_o selects the external target; any corruption misaddresses requests, causing reads from or writes to unintended devices/locations and silently corrupting system state.

Name: neorv32_xbus.xbus_dat_o
Description: Outputs 32-bit write data to the external bus for store/write transactions (driven from bus_req.data).
Justification: xbus_dat_o carries the value committed to peripherals/memory; if altered, the system stores incorrect data, breaking end-to-end correctness guarantees.

Name: neorv32_xbus.xbus_dat_i
Description: Receives 32-bit read data from the external device and is forwarded into the internal bus response (bus_rsp.data) when a transaction is pending.
Justification: xbus_dat_i becomes the CPU-visible read value; spoofing or corruption injects wrong data into architectural state, leading to silent malfunction.

Name: neorv32_xbus.xbus_we_o
Description: Single-bit signal indicating direction of transfer (read=0 / write=1) for external transactions, driven from bus_req.rw.
Justification: xbus_we_o encodes operation direction; a flip turns reads into unintended writes (or vice versa), causing data loss or missed updates.

Name: neorv32_xbus.xbus_sel_o
Description: 4-bit byte-enable mask signaling which bytes of the 32-bit word are active for the transfer (driven from bus_req.ben).
Justification: xbus_sel_o chooses active byte lanes; errors drop intended bytes or overwrite adjacent lanes, producing partial-word corruption.

Name: neorv32_xbus.xbus_stb_o
Description: Strobe asserting a valid single transfer request on the external bus (driven from bus_req.stb); part of the pipelined Wishbone interface.
Justification: xbus_stb_o launches transfers; if suppressed the bus never issues requests (stall), while if stuck high it can monopolize the bus or create protocol violations, degrading throughput.

Name: neorv32_xbus.xbus_cyc_o
Description: Indicates a valid bus cycle and is asserted while a transfer is active (driven as bus_req.stb OR pending(1)) to hold cycles across multi-step/atomic accesses.
Justification: xbus_cyc_o holds the bus during active/atomic sequences; incorrect assertion/deassertion can hang the bus or prematurely end cycles, stalling forward progress.

Name: neorv32_xbus.xbus_ack_i
Description: Acknowledge from the external device indicating transfer completion; used to generate bus_rsp.ack and to advance/complete pending transactions and atomic sequences.
Justification: xbus_ack_i completes handshakes; if missing, requests never retire (stall), while spurious acks can advance state without valid data, leading to retries or lockups.

Name: neorv32_xbus.xbus_err_i
Description: Error indication from the external device used to set bus_rsp.err and to abort/clear pending transactions and timeouts.
Justification: xbus_err_i terminates faulty transactions; if suppressed, the controller may wait indefinitely, and if asserted spuriously, valid traffic is aborted—both harming availability.

Name: neorv32_xbus.pending
Description: Internal two-bit vector tracking pending/atomic transaction state used to gate responses (bus_rsp) and to compute xbus_cyc_o behavior.
Justification: pending tracks in-flight/atomic sequences; corruption can keep the bus busy forever or drop cycles early, causing deadlock or lost operations.

Name: neorv32_xbus.timeout
Description: Internal flag asserted when the timeout counter reaches TIMEOUT_VAL to signal an unacknowledged access and produce a bus error/abort.
Justification: timeout bounds wait time; if never asserted, the bus can hang on unresponsive devices, and if asserted prematurely, valid transactions are aborted—both reducing availability.

Name: neorv32_xbus.timecnt
Description: Internal cycle counter that is incremented while a transaction is pending and compared against TIMEOUT_VAL to generate the timeout flag.
Justification: timecnt drives timeout detection; incorrect counting delays or prevents aborts (hang) or triggers early timeouts (spurious aborts), impairing forward progress.
